examples: There has been a growing awareness of the need for countries to get together to co-ordinate their policies.
Digital technology also makes it easier for people to co-ordinate their activities without resorting to hierarchy.
It might also be hard for airlines to co-ordinate the departure times and destinations of passenger aircraft in a way that would allow them to gain from formation flight.
It will also help to co-ordinate assistance from individual countries that choose to get involved.
Union leaders will hold another meeting at the end of September to co-ordinate their actions.
